evals.prompt: Read this client email and tell me what to do: 'Hey, hope you're well. Quick question, we've been having some intermittent reliability issues with the system lately and management is starting to ask questions. Can we hop on a call sometime this week to discuss our options? Nothing urgent, just want to understand what's possible. Thanks!'
evals.expected_output: Skill extracts: surface ask = call this week to discuss reliability options. Real ask underneath = the system is broken or unstable, management wants accountability. Underlying fear = looking bad to management, possible blame coming. Implicit constraints = needs to demo progress to management soon. Political context = management is escalating. What they did not ask = what changed recently, what 'intermittent' means in numbers, is this a regression. Recommended response shape includes asking what changed before agreeing to the call.

assertions.text: Skill distinguishes surface ask ('call to discuss') from real ask (system is unstable)
Skill identifies 'Reliable' / 'reliability issues' signal as 'previously burned' or 'currently broken'
Skill identifies 'nothing urgent' as likely contradicted by 'management is asking questions'
Skill flags 'can we hop on a call' as potential avoid-paper-trail or distrust signal
Skill produces 'what they did NOT ask' section with concrete missing details
Skill does NOT auto-draft a response, presents brief and asks for direction

evals.prompt: Process this RFP: '[15-paragraph generic RFP for AWS cost optimization consulting]'
evals.expected_output: Skill summarises chronologically if multi-section, extracts deadline, budget signals, scope ambiguity, decision-maker identity, and recommends response shape (proposal, intake call, or decline).

assertions.text: Skill extracts deadline/budget/scope from RFP language
Skill identifies who the decision-maker likely is
Skill flags any sections that look like template-padding vs. real signals
Skill recommends response format (proposal / call / decline)

evals.prompt: My buddy texted me 'yo can you grab milk on the way home?'
evals.expected_output: Skill declines to process. Tells the user this is a single-line clear ask; just answer.

assertions.text: Skill detects this is a one-line clear ask and declines to over-process
